hook: Real progression is easier to trust when it happens in the open, not in a haze of assumptions.
body: Talking plainly about pace, intent, and expectations can prevent a lot of unnecessary fantasy or panic.

hook: Avoiding the conversation does not keep things light; it often just makes both people invent their own private story.
body: A simple check-in can reduce pressure by replacing guesswork with actual information.

hook: A simple check-in can reduce pressure by replacing guesswork with actual information.
body: Asking how the other person is feeling about the direction of things can be cleaner than silently trying to infer it from every text.

body: When the connection matters, ask one straightforward question instead of running ten private interpretations.
